news.title: Emory Nursing launches Year 2 of its NIH-funded SDOH ART Fellowship
news.excerpt: Emory's Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing has launched the second year of its NIH-funded Advanced Research Training (ART) program. The 2026 SDOH ART Fellows receive mentored research training focused on the social determinants of health, transdisciplinary collaboration, and innovative methodologies — supporting early-career researchers and mid-career faculty across cancer, cardiovascular health, aging, climate change, and behavioral health. Directed by Dr. Jill Hamilton, the program aims to shape the next generation of health-equity leaders.

news.title: Emory School of Nursing taps researchers for social determinants of health fellowship
news.excerpt: The Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing named 14 nurse scientists and interdisciplinary researchers across the nation as its inaugural cohort of SDOH ART Fellows. The group gathered at Emory June 2–6, 2025 for faculty-led training on integrating social determinants of health into their research — spanning cancer, cardiovascular disease, aging and cognition, environmental toxins, and behavioral health, with resources from the school's Center for Data Science and Office of Nursing Research. The program is funded by an R25 grant from the National Institute of Nursing Research.
